message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: High-stakes subprocess sandbox settings (permission_mode/allowed_tools/setting_sources) are never asserted
DIMENSION: test quality / do tests actually guard behavior
SEVERITY: HIGH
FILE: tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py 53-159
DESCRIPTION: _high_stakes_complete builds ClaudeAgentOptions with permission_mode='dontAsk', setting_sources=[], allowed_tools=[] (seam.py:382-389) — the sandbox that stops the subscription subprocess from executing arbitrary tools or reading project/user settings. No drain test reads any of these option fields. VERIFIED non-discriminating: changing permission_mode to 'bypassPermissions' and allowed_tools to ['Bash'] (which would let the high-stakes Claude subprocess run arbitrary shell commands on the production node) passes all 12 drain tests. A regression weakening the sandbox would be invisible to CI.
SUGGESTED FIX: Capture the constructed ClaudeAgentOptions in the fixture and assert permission_mode == 'dontAsk', allowed_tools == [], and setting_sources == [] (and system_prompt/model are threaded through). Pair with the env assertion above so the whole ADR-0003 hardening of the subprocess is locked down by tests.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this finding by examining the actual code and tests.

content.content:
1	"""High-stakes (T3) stream-drain + error classification — exercised via synthetic SDK messages.
2	
3	The real `_high_stakes_complete._drain` and its 429/auth/error/empty classification is the most
4	dangerous untested code (it adjudicates high-stakes verdicts and maps subscription rate
5	windows). Validating it against the real frontier costs a metered call and only runs when
6	`ASSAY_RUN_HIGH_STAKES_TESTS=1`. Here we drive the REAL drain logic by injecting a fake
7	`claude_agent_sdk` whose `query` yields synthetic `AssistantMessage`/`RateLimitEvent`/
8	`ResultMessage` objects — covering the classification branches in CI with no metered call.
9	
10	Requires anyio (the reasoning extra is async); skips otherwise.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import sys
16	import types
17	
18	import pytest
19	
20	pytest.importorskip("anyio")
21	
22	from assay_engine.reasoning import seam as rc  # noqa: E402
23	from assay_engine.reasoning.seam import (  # noqa: E402
24	    PermanentReasoningError,
25	    RateLimitError,
26	    ReasoningError,
27	)
28	
29	
30	class _TextBlock:
31	    def __init__(self, text):
32	        self.text = text
33	
34	
35	class _AssistantMessage:
36	    def __init__(self, content):
37	        self.content = content
38	
39	
40	class _ResultMessage:
41	    def __init__(self, is_error=False, api_error_status=None, errors=None, stop_reason=None):
42	        self.is_error = is_error
43	        self.api_error_status = api_error_status
44	        self.errors = errors
45	        self.stop_reason = stop_reason
46	
47	
48	class RateLimitEvent:  # name matters: the engine matches by type(msg).__name__ (real SDK contract)
49	    def __init__(self, status):
50	        self.rate_limit_info = types.SimpleNamespace(status=status)
51	
52	
53	class _ClaudeAgentOptions:
54	    def __init__(self, **kw):
55	        self.__dict__.update(kw)
56	
57	
58	@pytest.fixture
59	def fake_sdk(monkeypatch):
60	    """Inject a fake claude_agent_sdk whose `query` yields a scripted message stream."""
61	    scripted: list = []
62	
63	    async def query(prompt, options):  # noqa: ANN001
64	        for msg in scripted:
65	            if isinstance(msg, Exception):  # simulate the SDK/transport raising mid-stream
66	                raise msg
67	            yield msg
68	
69	    mod = types.ModuleType("claude_agent_sdk")
70	    mod.AssistantMessage = _AssistantMessage
71	    mod.ResultMessage = _ResultMessage
72	    mod.TextBlock = _TextBlock
73	    mod.ClaudeAgentOptions = _ClaudeAgentOptions
74	    mod.query = query
75	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "claude_agent_sdk", mod)
76	    monkeypatch.setenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "test-subscription-token")
77	    return scripted
78	
79	
80	def _run(prompt="hi", system=None, model=None):
81	    return rc._high_stakes_complete(prompt, system, model)
82	
83	
84	def test_t3_success_collects_text_blocks(fake_sdk):
85	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("po"), _TextBlock("ng")]))
86	    assert _run() == "pong"
87	
88	
89	def test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient(fake_sdk):
90	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([]))  # no text blocks -> empty
91	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
92	        _run()
93	
94	
95	def test_t3_rate_limit_event_rejected_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
96	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
97	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
98	        _run()
99	
100	
101	def test_t3_rate_limit_allowed_is_not_an_error(fake_sdk):
102	    # an informational 'allowed' rate event must NOT trigger backpressure; text still returns
103	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("allowed"))
104	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("ok")]))
105	    assert _run() == "ok"
106	
107	
108	def test_t3_auth_error_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
109	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=401, errors=["unauth"]))
110	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
111	        _run()
112	
113	
114	def test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
115	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500, errors=["boom"]))
116	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
117	        _run()
118	
119	
120	def test_t3_429_not_clobbered_by_trailing_result_error(fake_sdk):
121	    # a RateLimitEvent(rejected) must win over a later ResultMessage error (preserve backpressure)
122	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
123	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500))
124	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
125	        _run()
126	
127	
128	def test_t3_overloaded_529_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
129	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=529, errors=["overloaded"]))
130	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
131	        _run()
132	
133	
134	def test_t3_forbidden_403_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
135	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=403, errors=["forbidden"]))
136	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
137	        _run()
138	
139	
140	def test_t3_top_level_rejected_status_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
141	    # a non-RateLimitEvent message carrying status=='rejected' is the second 429 path
142	    msg = types.SimpleNamespace(status="rejected")
143	    fake_sdk.append(msg)
144	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
145	        _run()
146	
147	
148	def test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
149	    # the SDK/transport raising (rather than completing the stream) is a transient failure
150	    fake_sdk.append(RuntimeError("connection reset"))
151	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
152	        _run()
153	
154	
155	def test_t3_missing_auth_fails_loud(monkeypatch, fake_sdk):
156	    monkeypatch.delenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", raising=False)
157	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "_high_stakes_auth_present", lambda: False)
158	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError, match="subscription auth"):
159	        _run()
160
/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py

340	            s in msg for s in ("not found", "no such model", "try pulling")
341	        )
342	        if permanent:
343	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
344	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
345	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
346	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
347	    text = text.strip()
348	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result — retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
349	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
350	    return text
351	
352	
353	def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
354	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
355	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
356	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
357	        return True
358	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()
359	
360	
361	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
362	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
363	    try:
364	        import anyio
365	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
366	            AssistantMessage,
367	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
368	            ResultMessage,
369	            TextBlock,
370	            query,
371	        )
372	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
373	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
374	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
375	        ) from exc
376	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
377	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
378	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
379	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
380	        )
381	
382	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
383	        system_prompt=system,
384	        model=model,
385	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
386	        setting_sources=[],
387	        allowed_tools=[],
388	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
389	    )
390	
391	    parts: list[str] = []
392	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
393	
394	    async def _drain() -> None:
395	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
396	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
397	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
398	        nonlocal result_error
399	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
400	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
401	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
402	                if rl == "rejected":
403	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
404	                continue
405	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
406	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
407	                continue
408	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
409	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
410	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
411	                        parts.append(block.text)
412	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
413	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
414	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
415	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
416	                ):
417	                    result_error = {
418	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
419	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
420	                    }
421	
422	    try:
423	        anyio.run(_drain)
424	    except Exception as exc:
425	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
426	
427	    if result_error is not None:
428	        status = result_error.get("status")
429	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
430	        if status in (429, 529):
431	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
432	        if status in (401, 403):
433	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
434	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
435	
436	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
437	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
438	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
439	    return text
440	
441	
442	def _attempt(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
443	    """Route one request to its tier backend (no retry here)."""
444	    if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
445	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning is disabled (ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING)")
446	    p = request.params
447	    system = p.get("system")
448	    temperature = float(p.get("temperature", 0.0))
449	    model = p.get("model")
450	    json_mode = bool(p.get("_json_mode", False))
451	    seed = p.get("_seed")
452	    json_schema = p.get("json_schema")  # optional caller schema → constrained JSON decoding
453	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
454	        return cast(
455	            str,
456	            _with_timeout(
457	                lambda: _bulk_complete(
458	                    request.prompt,
459	                    system,

content.text: The code confirms the sandbox settings. Now let me verify the claim that the drain tests don't assert these. The tests use a fake `_ClaudeAgentOptions` that just stores kwargs, and `query` ignores `options` entirely. Let me PoC the non-discriminating claim.

content.text: So `test_reasoning.py` only tests `scrubbed_env()` in isolation, not the option fields. Now let me run the PoC: weaken the sandbox in the fixture's options handling and confirm all 12 drain tests still pass. The real test: mutate `seam.py` to use `bypassPermissions` and `allowed_tools=['Bash']`, then run the drain tests.
12 passed baseline. Now the discriminating PoC: weaken the sandbox and re-run.

content.text:
Restored to safe values. My PoC is conclusive.

## Verification summary

**Finding is TRUE.**

Confirmed facts:
1. `seam.py:382-389` builds `ClaudeAgentOptions` with the sandbox: `permission_mode="dontAsk"`, `setting_sources=[]`, `allowed_tools=[]`, plus `env=scrubbed_env()`. This is the ADR-0003 hardening that prevents the subscription subprocess from running arbitrary tools or reading project/user `CLAUDE.md`/settings.
2. The drain test fixture (`test_reasoning_drain.py:53-77`) defines `_ClaudeAgentOptions` as a bag that just stores kwargs, and its fake `query(prompt, options)` **ignores `options` entirely** — it only iterates the scripted message list. So no option field is ever observed.
3. A grep across the whole `tests/` tree finds **zero** assertions on `permission_mode`, `allowed_tools`, or `setting_sources`. `test_reasoning.py` covers `scrubbed_env()` in isolation (the `env` field's value) but never the sandbox fields, and never asserts the options object that `_high_stakes_complete` actually constructs.

PoC (discriminating mutation):
- Baseline: all 12 drain tests pass.
- Mutated `seam.py` to `permission_mode="bypassPermissions"`, `setting_sources=["project","user"]`, `allowed_tools=["Bash"]` — a sandbox-defeating regression that would let the high-stakes Claude subprocess execute arbitrary shell on the production node and ingest project/user settings.
- Result: **all 12 drain tests still passed (`12 passed in 0.02s`).** The mutation is invisible to CI.
- Restored the file via `git checkout`.

This is a real test-quality / production-readiness defect: the security-critical sandbox configuration has no regression guard. The mutation tested is exactly the kind of plausible weakening (e.g. someone enabling tools to let the high-stakes tier "do more") that the test suite should catch and does not.

Severity recalibration: I agree with HIGH. It is not CRITICAL because the production code today is correct — this is a missing guard, not an active vulnerability. But on a data-sovereign production node where the subprocess runs under a real subscription, an unguarded sandbox config is the highest-consequence thing to leave untested; a silent regression would grant arbitrary local code execution with no CI signal. The assessor's suggested fix (capture the constructed options in the fixture and assert the three sandbox fields plus that `system_prompt`/`model` thread through, paired with an `env`/`scrubbed_env` assertion) is correct and sufficient.

One minor scope note for the parent: the fixture's `query` would need to capture the `options` passed to it (e.g. append to a list or stash on the fixture) so the test can read the real fields — asserting on a separately-constructed `_ClaudeAgentOptions` would not guard `seam.py`.

input.reasoning: The pass-2 finding is a genuine production-readiness defect in test quality. seam.py:382-389 constructs the high-stakes subprocess sandbox (permission_mode='dontAsk', setting_sources=[], allowed_tools=[], env=scrubbed_env()) — the ADR-0003 hardening that stops the subscription Claude subprocess from executing arbitrary tools or reading project/user settings on the data-sovereign production node. The drain test fixture's fake query(prompt, options) ignores the options object entirely and only iterates a scripted message list, so no option field is ever observed. A grep over the whole tests/ tree finds zero assertions on permission_mode, allowed_tools, or setting_sources; test_reasoning.py only covers scrubbed_env() in isolation, never the sandbox fields nor the constructed options object. I ran a discriminating PoC: with the code unmodified all 12 drain tests pass; after mutating seam.py to permission_mode='bypassPermissions', setting_sources=['project','user'], allowed_tools=['Bash'] (a sandbox-defeating regression that would grant the high-stakes subprocess arbitrary shell execution and project/user settings ingestion), all 12 drain tests STILL passed (12 passed in 0.02s). The mutation is invisible to CI. I restored the file via git checkout. Severity HIGH is correct (not CRITICAL: production code today is correct, this is a missing regression guard; not lower: an unguarded sandbox config on a production node is the highest-consequence untested surface — a silent regression would yield local code execution with no CI signal). Note for the fix: the fixture's query must capture the options actually passed to it so the test reads seam.py's real fields; asserting on a separately-built options object would not guard the seam.

PoC: baseline `pytest tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py -q` => 12 passed; after editing seam.py to permission_mode='bypassPermissions'/setting_sources=['project','user']/allowed_tools=['Bash'] => 12 passed in 0.02s (regression undetected); restored via `git checkout -- src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py`. content.text:
I have completed the verification and submitted my structured output.

**Conclusion: TRUE finding, severity HIGH (confirmed).**

The security-critical subprocess sandbox config (`permission_mode='dontAsk'`, `setting_sources=[]`, `allowed_tools=[]` at `seam.py:382-389`) has no test guard. I proved non-discrimination with a PoC: mutating the sandbox to `bypassPermissions`/`allowed_tools=['Bash']` (which would grant the high-stakes subprocess arbitrary local code execution on the production node) still passes all 12 drain tests. Production code itself is correct — this is a missing regression guard, not an active vulnerability — which is why HIGH rather than CRITICAL is appropriate. File restored via git checkout. The assessor's suggested fix is correct, with one implementation note: the fixture's fake `query` must capture the `options` it actually receives so the assertions guard `seam.py` rather than a separately-built object.
